seo.title: About Us
seo.description: ARG Software is a small architecture-first studio founded by José Antunes and Rui Rocha, built on technical trust, shared standards, and software that lasts.

title: A way of working,
before a company.
hero.subtitle: ARG Software is an architecture-first studio founded by José Antunes and Rui Rocha after years of working together across engineering, telecom, product, architecture, and international projects.

origin.title: ARG was built on technical trust and shared standards.
paragraphs: ARG Software was born from a long-standing professional relationship and a common belief: great software is not created by adding more people to a project, but by bringing the right people together.
Before becoming a company, ARG was already a way of working: precise, reliable, pragmatic, and deeply focused on quality. The company came later. The standard came first.

timeline.title: Where it started, and how ARG became a company.
timeline.intro: The story is not a pitch-deck origin. It is a working relationship tested across teams, clients, and production systems before ARG became official.

steps.headline: Production work before ARG had a name.
steps.summary: Both founders entered software through practical delivery, not abstract theory alone. The first years were already about real users, data-heavy products, public-facing systems, and business rules that had to work outside a demo.
highlights: José worked on InfoPortugal products, including VisitPortugal and real-time Traffic/GIS systems.
Rui built a financial management system by translating complex calculation sheets into a richer web application.
Both paths started with product work where interfaces, data models, deployment, and maintainability all mattered.
steps.whyItMattered: ARG's later standard comes from this kind of work: software has to survive real users, existing processes, and long-term ownership.

details.label: José
details.text: Built early experience across backend, web, mobile, maps, and public-facing platforms while completing Computer Science and Software Architecture studies at the University of Porto.

details.label: Rui
details.text: Moved from education into hands-on product delivery, including financial workflows, Salesforce-based systems, and client-facing application work.

steps.headline: Telecom put them in the same engineering culture.
steps.summary: At Celfinet, José and Rui worked around telecom software, dashboards, GIS systems, and internal tools where reliability and code quality were not optional. This is where the working relationship started to take shape.
highlights: Worked on software connected to telecom operators and network-performance products
Built and maintained dashboard, GIS, reporting, backoffice, and internal tooling across Angular, React, .NET, OpenLayers, GeoServer, and related stacks.
Operated in teams using code reviews, pair programming, testing, continuous integration, and refactoring of legacy systems.
steps.whyItMattered: This period created the technical trust behind ARG: shared standards, direct feedback, and the ability to work under production pressure without lowering the bar.

details.label: Together
details.text: Their paths crossed in demanding telecom work, where the quality of architecture, frontend execution, backend design, and operational tooling all affected delivery.

steps.headline: Ownership became part of the job, not a title.
steps.summary: After the telecom years, both founders moved into roles with more responsibility. José took stronger architecture and technical leadership positions; Rui expanded through agency and studio-style delivery, APIs, QA, deployment, and client-facing execution.
highlights: José worked on EQS Digital and the UNO industrial platform, taking responsibility for architecture, refactoring, coaching, code review, Scrum practices, and DDD/SOLID-oriented backend design.
Rui worked through PICUS Digital Web Agency, implementing frontend designs, creating APIs, adding unit, integration, and end-to-end test coverage.
Both paths reinforced the same habit: senior engineers should own the shape of the system, not just their assigned tickets.
steps.whyItMattered: ARG's delivery model depends on senior ownership. These years shaped the expectation that architecture, implementation, QA, CI/CD, and communication belong together.

details.label: José
details.text: Moved deeper into architecture and leadership, including ERP/industrial software work, team coaching, code review, Scrum guidance, and backend architecture patterns.

details.label: Rui
details.text: Built and shipped agency/studio projects across frontend, APIs, QA automation, deployment, and operational maintenance.

steps.headline: Remote product engineering across markets.
steps.summary: The founders kept growing through independent and international work. José focused on architecture-heavy platforms in the United States and Germany; Rui worked through freelance and product roles connected to scalable web applications and operational workflows.
highlights: José worked with North Music Group in Los Angeles on backend architecture, DDD/CQRS, Scrum coaching, CI/CD, and scalable music-industry software.
José also worked on Micron Manufacturing, an IoT production-management application with architecture, refactoring, CI/CD, and test practices.
Rui worked as a freelancer on scalable web applications and on Quindi's Portugal 2020 project-management platform, focused on finance/deadline approval workflows.
steps.whyItMattered: This period made ARG's remote-first operating model practical. The team learned how to align across time zones, domains, and technical stacks while keeping delivery accountable.

details.label: José
details.text: Worked on architecture-heavy international systems for music technology and manufacturing, with backend design, distributed delivery, and CI/CD responsibility.

details.label: Rui
details.text: Worked on scalable web applications and project-management software, carrying frontend, backend, requirements, and business-workflow implementation.

steps.headline: The collaboration became a company through real client work.
steps.summary: ARG was not formed around a slide deck. It grew out of active client delivery, shared standards, and the decision to formalize a way of working that was already producing results.
highlights: ARG began around software architecture, full-stack delivery, scalable systems, and direct founder involvement.
Early ARG work included Dokutar, a cloud-based documentation and compliance-oriented product for German companies.
The stack already reflected the studio's range: Node.js, TypeScript, React, Angular, .NET, PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Docker, AWS, CI/CD, and testing.
steps.whyItMattered: This is where ARG's positioning became concrete: small senior teams, architecture-first delivery, and software built to keep evolving after the first release.

details.label: ARG
details.text: The company formalized a proven collaboration model: direct communication, technical discipline, pragmatic delivery, and responsibility beyond handoff.

steps.headline: ARG moved from formation to a broader portfolio.
steps.summary: The studio expanded across music technology, financial technology, documentation products, and food-service software, while keeping the same pattern: senior delivery, architecture, code review, CI/CD, and maintainability.
highlights: Skytracks, based in Canada, brought cloud-based music production work across Node.js, Koa, RxJS, Angular, Jest, Docker, queues, and MySQL.
Mojaloop, worldwide open source initiative, added open-source payment infrastructure work involving TypeScript, Express, Kafka, Angular, Docker, MongoDB, Elasticsearch, and Kibana.
Leeroy, a company based in Stockholm, bringing a PWA for food service, using React, Storybook, Framer Motion, GraphQL, Fastify, MongoDB, Redis, AWS, Docker, Argo CD, GitHub Actions, and Datadog.
steps.whyItMattered: This period showed that ARG's model could transfer across domains without becoming generic: the work changed, but the engineering standard stayed the same.

details.label: ARG
details.text: ARG proved its studio model across client products that required backend systems, frontend applications, distributed workflows, and disciplined delivery practices.

steps.headline: Growth through trust, not headcount.
steps.summary: ARG today is a small software studio built around senior ownership and carefully selected collaborators. The company does not scale by adding people for optics; it scales by assembling trusted capability around the problem.
highlights: Current and past ARG clients include AngryVentures, Hopupon, Hostelier, MB-Netzwerk, Mojaloop Foundation, North Music Group, SEFA, SkyTracks, and others.

The work spans payment infrastructure, music technology, travel, hospitality, documentation, SaaS products, backend systems, frontend applications, cloud infrastructure, and AI-enabled product thinking.
The operating model remains consistent: architecture, code review, testing, CI/CD, direct communication, and accountability from discovery to production.
steps.whyItMattered: This is the company ARG intentionally became: selective, technical, direct, and built around people trusted to own the work properly.

details.label: ARG
details.text: ARG consolidates its presence in the market by selectively bringing in excellent collaborators, keeping the non-negotiable commitment that quality always wins over quantity.

founders.title: Two paths. One engineering standard.
founders.intro: Different journeys into software, held to the same bar. Neither leads alone; both carry the work.

people.name: José Antunes
people.bio: Computer Science and an M.Sc. in Software Architecture from the University of Porto. Worked across backend, frontend, mobile, full-stack development, architecture, and technical leadership.
people.focus: Architecture, scalable systems, and technical leadership

people.name: Rui Rocha
people.bio: Computer Engineering from ISEP. Built his path through freelance work, telecom, product delivery, and a programming studio serving international clients and national brands.
people.focus: Product-minded delivery, frontend, mobile, and execution

company.title: From collaboration to company.
paragraphs: ARG was created in 2020 to formalize a way of working that already existed between the founders: direct communication, technical discipline, clear ownership, and a high standard for delivery.

The goal was never to become a large vendor. It was to help companies build software that can grow with the business, using the right team for the problem instead of adding people for the sake of scale.

points.title: Technical trust
points.description: Earned across shared projects, production pressure, and years of working together.

points.title: Complementary skills
points.description: Architecture, product thinking, delivery, and engineering execution covering each other’s blind spots.

points.title: A shared definition of done
points.description: Reliable software, clear communication, maintainable systems, and ownership beyond handoff.

beliefs.title: What we believe.
beliefs.intro: Long-term quality beats short-term volume. ARG is intentionally selective about the work we take and the people we bring into it.

principles.title: Quality over quantity
principles.description: Great software is not made by adding bodies. We assemble the right capability for the work in front of us.

principles.title: Trusted collaborators
principles.description: We work with people we trust, people who care about the craft, and people who know how to take ownership.

principles.title: Software built to last
principles.description: Technical discipline, clear communication, and maintainability matter because production systems outlive the first release.

collaborators.title: We grow by trust, not headcount.
paragraphs: ARG is more than two founders. Around the partnership is a network of excellent collaborators: people we have worked with, trust, and bring in when the work calls for their expertise.

The team combines backend systems, frontend applications, mobile development, cloud infrastructure, software architecture, product development, and technical leadership. More importantly, it combines people who know how to work together.
